payload.message:
Run the MOR staking session monitor.

CRITICAL: Always close stale sessions BEFORE opening new ones.

Steps:
1. Run cleanup first: bash ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/everclaw/scripts/session.sh cleanup
   - This enumerates ALL on-chain sessions via Diamond contract pagination
   - Closes stale/expired sessions (keeps latest per model)
   - The proxy-router /sessions/user has a hidden limit (~100 sessions) — cleanup uses on-chain pagination to find ALL sessions

2. Check wallet balance: bash ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/everclaw/scripts/session.sh status

3. For each target model in MORPHEUS_TARGET_MODELS:
   - Check if there is an active session (from cleanup output)
   - If no active session, open one: bash scripts/session.sh open <model> 86400
   - If session exists and expires within 2 hours, open a new one

4. Report results via YOUR_CHANNEL to YOUR_PHONE:
   - Active sessions (model, expiry)
   - Sessions cleaned up (count, MOR freed)
   - Wallet balance (MOR + ETH)
   - Any failures

Environment variables required:
  MORPHEUS_WALLET_ADDRESS - proxy-router wallet
  MORPHEUS_TARGET_MODELS  - e.g. 'glm-5 glm-5.1:web'

After sending the report: NO_REPLY

Quick MOR balance check. Run: bash ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/everclaw/scripts/session.sh status

Parse the output:
- If MOR balance < 500, alert via YOUR_CHANNEL to YOUR_PHONE: '⚠️ Low MOR: [X] MOR in wallet. Need ~1268 per session.'
- If ETH balance < 0.005, alert: '⚠️ Low ETH for gas: [X] ETH.'
- If everything looks fine, respond with NO_REPLY.
